text: Hi, I am Dr. Şaban Beyazpınar, in Turkey. This case is an explanatory video of laparoscopic machial diverticulaectomy with linear stapler during laparoscopic appendectomy surgery. Our clinic case is a thirty-eight years old Turkish male patient. Since three years there have been occasionally abdominal pains and nausea. Intraoperative evaluation, right lower quadrant head pain and tenderness as well as rebound tenderness. Acute phase reactants are high. Local abdominal ultrasonography revealed local peritonitis including local ileus minimally fluid around the intestine and suspicious diverticula. Lower abdominal CT revealed diverticula.

It was decided that appendicitis or the verticillitis could be diagnosed with acute abdomen and operation was obtained. After preparatively preparations, umbilical, ten millimeter, left lower quadrant, five cubic, five millimeters. Ports were placed and operated under a pressure of ten millimeters. The patient's position, Drendelenburg, in the spine position. The patient's position, the location of the ports and the location of the surgical team are showing in the diagrams.

The laparoscopic camera provides a view of the abdominal cavity, where the surgeon places 5mm ports in the left lower quadrant and suprapubic area. The appendix is revealed and identified, then ligated and excised using surgical instruments.

The surgeon ligates the appendix twice using an end-loop with a number zero V-create, completing the appendectomy. The appendix is extracted through the ten-millimeter camera port. The abdominal cavity and small intestine are explored and visualized, revealing a Meckel's diverticulum in the terminal ileum. A five-millimeter port in the bottom left quadrant is replaced with a twelve-millimeter port at the same incision site.

The surgeon excises the Meckel's diverticulum using a linear laparoscopic stapler. The excised diverticulum is removed through the twelve-millimeter port. The operation concludes with bleeding control and aspiration of the abdominal cavity.

Patient was discharged with minimal pain on the first postoperative day. On the seventh day, port's tooth were taken. Surgical complication was not observed. One month later, it was seen again. Nutrition and health problem did not occur. He got rid of his pain. Pathology revealed gastric mucosa within the inner surface make us diverticulum and acute sporadic appendicitis for appendix. Thank you for all.
